protocol: ACLS Guidelines (Door-to-Balloon/PCI, Thrombolysis)
time_window: 90 minutes for PCI; 30 minutes for thrombolysis (if PCI not available)

protocol: Surviving Sepsis Campaign (Bundle Care: IV fluids, vasopressors, antibiotics, lactate measurement)
time_window: 1 hour for bundle initiation (fluids, antibiotics, lactate)

protocol: AHA/ASA Stroke Guidelines (tPA for ischemic, BP management for hemorrhagic)
time_window: 4.5 hours for tPA from symptom onset (ischemic); immediate BP control for hemorrhagic

protocol: Insulin Infusion Protocol, Aggressive Fluid Resuscitation, Electrolyte Correction
time_window: Immediate fluid and insulin therapy, continuous monitoring

protocol: Epinephrine administration (IM), Airway management, Antihistamines, Corticosteroids
time_window: Immediate epinephrine administration

protocol: Anticoagulation, Thrombolysis (if massive PE), Oxygen support, Hemodynamic support
time_window: STAT diagnosis and initiation of anticoagulation/thrombolysis

protocol: ACLS Guidelines (CPR, Defibrillation, Advanced Airway, Medications, Targeted Temperature Management)
time_window: Immediate (within minutes) for CPR and defibrillation

protocol: Aggressive Bronchodilators, Systemic Steroids, Oxygen, Magnesium Sulfate, Intubation (if needed)
time_window: Immediate assessment and aggressive therapy

protocol: IV Antihypertensives (titrated), Close Monitoring, Identify/Treat End-Organ Damage
time_window: Reduce BP within minutes to hours depending on presence of end-organ damage

protocol: Neurosurgical consultation, ICP management, Airway protection, Fluid management
time_window: Immediate assessment, CT head, and neurosurgical intervention if indicated

protocol: Fluid resuscitation, Blood transfusion (if needed), Endoscopy/Colonoscopy, PPI therapy
time_window: Immediate stabilization and urgent diagnostic evaluation/intervention

protocol: Fluid resuscitation (Parkland formula), Airway management, Pain control, Wound care, Burn center transfer
time_window: Immediate assessment, ABCs, and initiation of burn care

protocol: Spinal immobilization, Neurosurgical/Orthopedic consultation, Methylprednisolone (controversial), Airway/Respiratory support
time_window: Immediate immobilization and transfer to trauma center/specialized unit

protocol: Naloxone administration, Respiratory support (bag-valve mask/intubation), BLS/ACLS
time_window: Immediate naloxone administration and respiratory support

protocol: Decontamination (activated charcoal, gastric lavage, whole bowel irrigation), Antidote administration (if available), Supportive care (airway, breathing, circulation)
time_window: Immediate identification of substance and initiation of appropriate management

protocol: Ophthalmological emergency, Medications to lower intraocular pressure (topical, systemic), Laser iridotomy/surgical iridectomy
time_window: Immediate treatment to prevent permanent vision loss (within hours)

protocol: Surgical emergency (laparoscopy/laparotomy), Fluid resuscitation, Blood transfusion (if needed)
time_window: Immediate surgical intervention

protocol: Immediate BP and heart rate control, Surgical consultation for Type A, Medical management for Type B
time_window: STAT diagnosis and immediate medical/surgical management

protocol: ABCs, Benzodiazepines (first-line), Antiepileptic drugs (second-line), EEG monitoring
time_window: Immediate termination of seizure activity (within 5-10 minutes)

protocol: IV calcium (for hyperkalemia), Insulin/glucose, Diuretics, Fluid restriction/hypertonic saline (for hyponatremia), Dialysis (if severe)
time_window: Immediate correction of life-threatening electrolyte levels
